action.reasoning: Observation: Training completely diverged - loss grew from 3 to 110 linearly, all batches NaN by end. Best checkpoint is still from iter 2 (worst_region=0.3675). Analysis: The checkpoint from iter 2 had NaN weights (training was broken with float16+GradScaler). Resuming from broken weights + wrong AMP config caused catastrophic divergence. Need to: 1) Start fresh from CLIP pretrained weights (no resume), 2) Use float32 (no AMP) to avoid all NaN issues, 3) Use very conservative LR for backbone (1e-6), 4) Freeze backbone initially and only train head for first few epochs. Decision: Rewrite train.py completely - start from scratch CLIP weights, float32, freeze backbone for first 3 epochs then unfreeze with tiny LR, use cosine schedule.
